commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR maps Telegram local Bot API container and relative `file_path` values through configured `trustedLocalFileRoots` before falling back to HTTP downloads, with resolver regression tests.

PR surface: Source +96, Tests +114. Total +210 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main rejects container-absolute local Bot API paths that are outside the configured host trusted root, and the PR body includes production proof that mapped paths materialized documents. I did not run a live Telegram container in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve the useful mapping while fixing the catch-all trusted-root read fallback and adding one regression test.

target.title: fix(telegram): resolve local Bot API container file paths against trustedLocalFileRoots [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Self-hosted `telegram-bot-api` servers running with `--local` in a container report **absolute container paths** (`/var/lib/telegram-bot-api/<token>/...`) in `getFile`, while the OpenClaw host mounts that data volume somewhere else. The absolute path never matches a `trustedLocalFileRoots` entry, so inbound documents fail to materialize and reach the agent as bare `<media:document>` / `[file_id:...]` placeholders without bytes.
- This PR maps container-absolute `file_path` values back to relative candidates and resolves them under each trusted root, trying both with and without the per-token directory segment the local server uses on disk.
- Relative `file_path` values are now also resolved against trusted roots before falling back to the HTTP `file/bot<token>/` endpoint, which keeps local-mode large-file (>20MB) downloads working when the HTTP route cannot serve them.
- Out of scope: any change for setups without `trustedLocalFileRoots` (behavior unchanged — the new candidate loop only iterates configured roots).
- Reviewers: focus on the path-normalization guards in `normalizeTrustedTelegramRelativeFilePath` (NUL bytes, dot-segments, absolute escapes rejected) and on candidate ordering in `downloadAndSaveTelegramFile`.

## Linked context

Maintainer request: none — bug found operating a production OpenClaw gateway with the official `aiogram/telegram-bot-api` local server (needed for Telegram documents above the 20MB Bot API download limit).

Related # (none known)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: inbound Telegram documents (zip sent to a bot backed by a local `--local` Bot API server in Docker) never materialized into `media/inbound`; agents only saw `<media:document>` placeholders.
- Real environment tested: production OpenClaw 2026.6.1 gateway (Node 22.22.2, Ubuntu) + `aiogram/telegram-bot-api:latest` in Docker with `TELEGRAM_LOCAL=1`, data volume mounted at a host path configured in `trustedLocalFileRoots`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: sent a 499KB `.zip` document to the bot in a group topic; the gateway resolved `getFile`, mapped the container path to the trusted host root, and saved the file.
- Evidence after fix (redacted): inbound file materialized as `/root/.openclaw/media/inbound/king-new-dawn_1---<uuid>.zip` and the downstream agent flow completed with a real `operation=sendDocument deliveryKind=document` outbound journal line for the produced artifact.
- Observed result after fix: document bytes available to the agent; placeholder-only behavior gone.
- What was not tested: Windows hosts; non-Docker local servers using a custom `--dir`.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the live validation was done with this same logic applied as a dist-level hotfix on the running gateway (same code paths); this PR is the source port of that hotfix plus regression tests.
- Before evidence: gateway logs showed the message reaching the agent with `<media:document>`/`[file_id:...]` only, and `media/inbound` stayed empty for those messages.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.resolve-media-retry.test.ts` — 35 passed (31 existing + 4 new).
- New regression coverage: container-absolute path mapping, per-token directory resolution for relative paths, remote fallback when no candidate matches, and the existing outside-trusted-roots rejection preserved.
- What failed before this fix: container-absolute paths threw `MediaFetchError: ... outside trustedLocalFileRoots`; relative paths on local servers were fetched over HTTP only (failing for >20MB files).

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — inbound media on local Bot API setups now materializes instead of failing.
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No (same `trustedLocalFileRoots` key, no new config).
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? File reads stay behind the existing trusted-roots sandbox; new candidates are normalized and reject NUL/dot-segment/absolute escapes.
What is the highest-risk area? Path candidate construction accidentally widening what counts as "trusted".
How is that risk mitigated? Candidates are only ever resolved relative to operator-configured `trustedLocalFileRoots`, go through the same `fsRoot().read()` sandbox as the existing direct match, and traversal primitives are rejected before any read.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Maintainer review.
What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing on author side; happy to add more cases if requested.
Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? None yet (initial submission).
Note: companion PR #91986 had its real-behavior-proof evidence expanded with copied live output on 2026-06-10; the live timeline there also exercises this fix (the inbound zip only materialized after this path mapping was applied).

---
AI-assisted (Claude Code, reviewed and validated by the operator before submission).
target.author: dizesales
